title: Public Notices
description: This policy governs the requirements and procedures for public notices issued by the organization. It covers how and when official notices must be communicated to the public, ensuring transparency in organizational actions and decisions.

timeline_local.summary: This update creates a new Local Policy GC (Public Notices) for Conroe ISD, adding an entirely new "Digital Newspaper" section that establishes the criteria a digital newspaper must meet for the District to use it for publishing public notices. The policy is driven by Texas Senate Bill 1062, which amended Government Code § 2051.0441 to allow digital newspapers as an alternative publication venue.
changes_added: The District may utilize a digital newspaper to publish public notices as long as the digital newspaper:
2. Has been in business for at least three years;
3. Employs staff who are residents from within the District's geographic area;
4. Reports on local events and governmental activities in the District's geographic area;
5. Provides news of general interest to residents from within the District's geographic area; and
6. Updates its news at least once each week.
timeline_local.notes: This appears to be a brand-new local policy (Version 1, no prior text shown), so all red-underlined text represents new additions with no deletions. The cover page title reads "Consider and Adopt Revisions to Board Policy GC (Local)" — no "First Reading" language — so action is recorded as "adopted." The legislative basis document (page 3) confirms the change stems from Texas Senate Bill 1062 amending Tex. Gov't Code § 2051.0441. Page 4 appears blank/logo only and contains no additional policy text.

elements.element: Digital Newspaper — section heading and general authorization to use digital newspapers for public notices
elements.statutory_basis: TEX. GOV'T CODE § 2051.0441(b-1) (as amended by S.B. 1062, 89th Leg.)
elements.supporting_quote: In lieu of publishing a notice in a newspaper under Subsection (b), a governmental entity may publish a notice in a digital newspaper if that digital newspaper meets the criteria set out in this subsection.
elements.verification_method: verbatim statute text — S.B. 1062 enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov (§2051.0441(b-1) as added by S.B. 1062)
elements.local_additions: The statute creates an optional alternative to traditional newspaper publication; the district's decision to adopt a local policy exercising that option is a local choice, but the authorization itself comes directly from §2051.0441(b-1).

elements.verification_method: verbatim statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(1) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov
elements.local_additions: Verbatim statutory match. Policy criterion 1 corresponds to §2051.0441(b-1)(1). Order matches.

elements.supporting_quote: has been in business for at least three years
elements.verification_method: verbatim statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(2) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov
elements.local_additions: Verbatim statutory match. Policy criterion 2 corresponds to §2051.0441(b-1)(2). Order matches.

elements.element: Criterion 3 — staff who are residents from within the District's geographic area
elements.supporting_quote: employs staff in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity
elements.verification_method: adapted statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(3) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov
elements.local_additions: DEVIATION: The statute says 'employs staff in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity.' The policy says 'employs staff who are residents from within the District's geographic area.' Adding a residency requirement — staff must live in the area, not merely work there — is a local addition that goes beyond the statutory criterion and could be more restrictive than state law requires.

elements.element: Criterion 4 — reports on local events and governmental activities in the District's geographic area
elements.supporting_quote: reports on local events and governmental activities in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity
elements.verification_method: adapted statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(4) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov
elements.local_additions: 'District's geographic area' substituted for 'jurisdiction of the governmental entity.' Functionally equivalent; not verbatim.

elements.element: Criterion 5 — provides news of general interest to residents from within the District's geographic area
elements.supporting_quote: provides news of general interest to people in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity
elements.verification_method: adapted statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(5) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov
elements.local_additions: 'residents from within the District's geographic area' substituted for 'people in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity.' Adds 'residents from within' framing consistent with the broader residency theme in the policy, but the statute simply says 'people in the jurisdiction.'

elements.element: Criterion 6 — updates its news at least once each week
elements.supporting_quote: updates its news at least once each week
elements.verification_method: verbatim statute text — §2051.0441(b-1)(6) as added by S.B. 1062, enrolled bill text, 89th Leg., R.S., capitol.texas.gov

compliance_summary: Digital Newspaper Authorization — Permitted by Law: Texas S.B. 1062 (89th Leg., R.S.) amended Government Code §2051.0441 to add subsection (b-1), which allows governmental entities to publish public notices in a digital newspaper as an alternative to a traditional print newspaper, provided the digital newspaper meets six specific statutory criteria. Conroe ISD's policy exercises this option. The authorization is statutory, not locally invented.

Criteria 1, 2, and 6 — Verbatim Statutory Language: The requirements for an audited paid-subscriber base (criterion 1), at least three years in business (criterion 2), and weekly news updates (criterion 6) are taken verbatim from §2051.0441(b-1). The order of criteria in the policy differs from the statutory order but the language is identical.

Criterion 3 — Residency Deviation: The statute requires the digital newspaper to 'employ staff in the jurisdiction of the governmental entity.' The policy instead requires staff who are 'residents from within the District's geographic area.' This adds a residency requirement not present in the statute — staff must live in the area, not merely work there. This is a local addition that exceeds the statutory criterion.

Criteria 4 and 5 — Adapted Language: The policy substitutes 'District's geographic area' for 'jurisdiction of the governmental entity' in the local news coverage and general interest criteria. The meaning is functionally equivalent but the language is not verbatim.

Overall Classification Corrected: The prior assessment classified all elements as Locally Discretionary and marked all statutory quotes as unverified because the Claude API failed to extract the statutory text from the TEE legislative basis column. The statutory text of §2051.0441(b-1) was present in the source document and has now been applied. The correct overall classification is Permitted by Law.
